name: Current Affairs in History & National Movement (5): 86th Anniversary of the Forward Bloc's Nagpur Conference

sourceRefs.label: insightsonindia.com/vajiramandravi.com - 86th-anniversary commemoration (June 2026) of the All India Forward Bloc's first plenary conference, Nagpur, June 1940

researchNotes: Fifth History/National-Movement current-affairs topic (5th lap), in the same style as the Day-74 Vande Mataram anniversary topic — a dated commemoration anchoring a deep dive into real National Movement history. Note: sources disagree on the exact conference dates (18-22 June vs. 20-22 June 1940) — flagged transparently rather than presenting false precision. reading.intro: Covers the All India Forward Bloc's founding and its first plenary conference at Nagpur (June 1940) — marking its 86th anniversary in June 2026.

qa.question: Who founded the All India Forward Bloc, and when?
qa.answer: Subhas Chandra Bose, in May 1939 — after he resigned as Indian National Congress President (following conflict with the Gandhian leadership after the Tripuri Congress session) and formed Forward Bloc initially as a left-wing faction within the Congress.

qa.question: Where and when was the Forward Bloc's first plenary conference held, marking its 86th anniversary in 2026?
qa.answer: At Nagpur, in June 1940 (sources vary on the exact dates — either 18-22 or 20-22 June 1940).

qa.question: What was the significance of the Nagpur plenary conference for the Forward Bloc?
qa.answer: It transformed the Forward Bloc from a Congress faction into an INDEPENDENT socialist political party, committed to a militant, anti-imperialist programme.

qa.question: What was the key resolution/slogan that emerged from the Nagpur conference?
qa.answer: The resolution 'All Power to the Indian People' — Bose's rallying cry moving past standard constitutional demands toward urging militant action against British colonial rule.

qa.question: What eventually became of Subhas Chandra Bose's political trajectory after founding the Forward Bloc, connecting this topic to already-covered facts?
qa.answer: He was later placed under house arrest by the British, escaped in 1941, and eventually led the Indian National Army (INA)/Azad Hind Fauj in armed struggle against British rule from abroad — a continuation of the militant path signalled by the Forward Bloc's 'All Power to the Indian People' resolution.

questions.prompt: The All India Forward Bloc was founded by Subhas Chandra Bose in:
The Forward Bloc's first plenary conference (its 86th anniversary fell in June 2026) was held at:
The Nagpur plenary conference (June 1940) transformed the Forward Bloc into:
options: A wing of the Muslim League
An independent socialist, anti-imperialist political party
questions.explanation: An independent socialist, anti-imperialist political party.

questions.prompt: What rallying resolution emerged from the Nagpur conference?
options: All Power to the Indian People
questions.explanation: All Power to the Indian People.

questions.prompt: Subhas Chandra Bose resigned as Congress President and formed the Forward Bloc following conflict after which Congress session?
After his Forward Bloc years, Bose later led which armed organisation against British rule?
